title: Risks of Giardia infection (giardiasis) - Canada.ca
content:
## Risks of Giardia infection (giardiasis)
Learn about the risks of Giardia infection and who is most at  risk.
- Risks of getting Giardia infection

## Risks  of getting Giardia infection
Your risk of getting Giardia infection is highest if you come into contact with contaminated:
- infected animals, such as pets and livestock
Canadian drinking  water provided by municipalities is safe as there are systems in place to  protect Canadians from Giardia infection.
Giardia can infect  all people although there are some people that are more at risk, such as:
- people who eat contaminated food, such as fish
- people who care for those sick with Giardia infection
- children (up to 9 years old) in daycare centres and  schools
- people who are exposed to human feces during sexual  contact
- hikers, campers or backpackers who may drink or come into  contact with untreated water
- people who travel to countries, especially developing  countries, where Giardia infection may  be common
Complications such  as dehydration can occur  in rare cases.
